vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Minimum Special Characters
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's ocredit= parameter controls requirements for usage of special (or "other") characters in a password. When set to a negative number, any password will be required to contain that many special characters. When set to a positive number, pam_pwquality will grant +1 additional length credit for each special character. Modify the ocredit setting in /etc/security/pwquality.conf to equal to require use of a special character in passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then var_password_pam_ocredit='' if grep -sq ocredit /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/ocredit/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^ocredit") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s = %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_password_pam_ocredit" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_ALL=C grep -

triage_decision.reason: Password complexity rule adjustment via pam_pwquality is a standard hardening change; modifying ocredit does not affect existing sessions and can be validated before enforcement.

review_feedback: Remediation removes ocredit without explicitly setting a requirement to enforce special characters, so it does not fully satisfy the vulnerability. Suggestions: Set ocredit to a negative value (e.g., -1) or another appropriate setting to mandate at least one special character.; Add verification commands (e.g., test with a password containing no special characters) to ensure the rule is enforced.; Make the modified configuration files immutable or monitor them to prevent overwriting by packages.; Document the intended password policy and ensure all relevant parameters (ucredit, lcredit, dcredit, minlen) remain consistent.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Remediation removes ocredit without explicitly setting a requirement to enforce special characters, so it does not fully satisfy the vulnerability.
concerns: The fix deletes the ocredit directive rather than setting a concrete value that enforces a minimum number of special characters.
This may weaken password complexity if other rules do not compensate.
Package updates could reintroduce the setting, making the change fragile.
The remediation lacks an explicit verification step to confirm the desired PAM behavior.
suggested_improvements: Set ocredit to a negative value (e.g., -1) or another appropriate setting to mandate at least one special character.
Add verification commands (e.g., test with a password containing no special characters) to ensure the rule is enforced.
Make the modified configuration files immutable or monitor them to prevent overwriting by packages.
Document the intended password policy and ensure all relevant parameters (ucredit, lcredit, dcredit, minlen) remain consistent.
